name: Web UI login page
description: Add a login page that posts to /v1/session/login. Redirects to / on success. All protected pages, when they get a 401 from the API, redirect here. A simple 'Log out' button in the existing layout nav calls /v1/session/logout.
acceptance_criteria: web/src/app/login/page.tsx renders username + password inputs and a submit button; posts credentials JSON to /v1/session/login with credentials: 'include'
On success, redirects to the 'next' query param if safe (same origin, starts with '/') else '/'
On failure, shows the server's error message inline without leaving the page
web/src/lib/api.ts is updated to send credentials: 'include' on all requests and to redirect to /login?next=<current_path> when any response is 401
web/src/components/layout/ adds a small user menu (or inline button) with 'Log out' that posts to /v1/session/logout and then redirects to /login
When SESSION_SECRET is unset (open mode), hitting /login is still allowed but the submit redirects to / immediately since auth is disabled
pnpm --dir web lint, pnpm --dir web type-check, and pnpm --dir web build pass
Manual check against a running backend with SESSION_SECRET set: can log in, session persists across reloads, logout works
notes: Implemented on branch agent/web-ui-login-page. pnpm lint/type-check/build all pass. Root smoke.sh has preexisting failures (it expects npx eslint/tsc at repo root, but those tools live under web/node_modules); backend vet and fmt pass. Manual verification against a running backend is the remaining item in the acceptance criteria.
